subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **1** game board (world map with 42 territories in 6 continents)
- **6** sets of army pieces (Infantry, Cavalry, Artillery in 6 colors)
- **56** Risk cards (42 territory cards + 2 wild cards)
- **2** dice (red attack dice)
- **3** dice (white defense dice)
- **1** Golden Cavalry piece (first territory card trade-in bonus marker)
- **Mission cards** (optional variant)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place the board** — Unfold the game board showing the world map.
2. **Separate armies** — Each player takes one complete set of colored army pieces.
3. **Deal territory cards** — Shuffle the 42 territory cards and deal them evenly. Each player places one Infantry on each territory they received.
4. **Place remaining armies** — Starting armies depend on player count:
   - **2 players** — 40 armies each
   - **3 players** — 35 armies each
   - **4 players** — 30 armies each
   - **5 players** — 25 armies each
   - **6 players** — 20 armies each
5. **Deploy remaining** — After initial placement, players take turns placing remaining armies one at a time on owned territories.
6. **Shuffle Risk deck** — Collect territory cards back, add wilds, shuffle for draw pile.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Choose color** — Each player selects an army color.
- **Count armies** — Take starting armies based on player count.
- **Claim territories** — Place 1 army on each dealt territory.
- **Fortify** — Place remaining armies on owned territories in turn order.
- **Army equivalents** — 1 Cavalry = 5 Infantry, 1 Artillery = 10 Infantry.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Risk is a classic strategy game of global conquest. Players deploy armies, attack enemy territories, and attempt to complete their objective — either world domination (control all 42 territories) or a secret mission depending on variant.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each turn has 3 phases:

#### 1. Reinforce
- **Territory bonus** — Receive armies equal to total territories owned ÷ 3 (minimum 3).
- **Continent bonus** — If you control an entire continent, receive its bonus:
  - Asia: 7, North America: 5, Europe: 5, Africa: 3, South America: 2, Australia: 2
- **Card trade-in** — Trade matching sets of Risk cards for bonus armies (escalating value).
- **Place armies** — Deploy all new armies on any owned territories.

#### 2. Attack (Optional)
- **Declare attack** — Choose an owned territory with 2+ armies to attack an adjacent enemy territory.
- **Attacker rolls** — Up to 3 red dice (must have at least 1 more army than dice rolled).
- **Defender rolls** — Up to 2 white dice (must have at least as many armies as dice rolled).
- **Compare dice** — Match highest vs highest, then second highest vs second highest. Ties go to defender.
- **Remove casualties** — Loser of each comparison removes 1 army.
- **Conquer** — If defender loses all armies, attacker moves in (at least as many as dice rolled).
- **Draw card** — If you conquered at least 1 territory this turn, draw 1 Risk card.

#### 3. Fortify (Optional)
- **Move armies** — Move any number of armies from one owned territory to one connected owned territory.
- **One move only** — You may only make one fortify move per turn.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Card Sets for Trade-in
- **3 of a kind** — 3 Infantry, 3 Cavalry, or 3 Artillery cards.
- **One of each** — 1 Infantry + 1 Cavalry + 1 Artillery.
- **Wild cards** — A wild card can substitute for any type.
- **Escalating value** — 1st set: 4 armies, 2nd: 6, 3rd: 8, 4th: 10, 5th: 12, 6th: 15, then +5 each.
- **Territory bonus** — If a traded card matches a territory you own, place 2 extra armies there.
- **Mandatory trade** — Must trade if holding 5+ cards at start of turn.

#### Combat Details
- **Blitz option** — Some versions allow rolling repeatedly until attacker stops or territory is conquered.
- **Retreat** — Attacker may stop attacking at any time.
- **Capital rule** (variant) — Some editions include capital cities for bonus armies.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Elimination
- When you eliminate a player, you take all their Risk cards.
- If this gives you 6+ cards, you must immediately trade in sets.

#### 2-Player Variant
- A neutral third army is placed on the board.
- Each player attacks neutral territories as normal but neutral armies never attack.

#### Mission Variant
- Each player draws a secret mission card at start.
- First player to complete their mission wins (instead of total domination).

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Victory
- **World domination** — Control all 42 territories.

#### Mission Victory
- Complete your secret mission card objective.

#### Timed Game Variant
- Set a time limit; player controlling most territories when time expires wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Hold a continent** — Australia is easiest to defend (1 entry point, 2 bonus armies).
- **Don't spread thin** — Concentrate armies rather than spreading 1 per territory.
- **Attack to earn cards** — Conquer at least 1 territory per turn to draw a Risk card.
- **Defend chokepoints** — Place extra armies on continent borders.
- **Watch card timing** — Trade in cards at optimal moments for maximum army surges.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Continent priority** — Australia first, then South America or Africa for manageable defense.
- **Turtle vs aggression** — Turtling builds strength but lets opponents grow; balance defense with selective strikes.
- **Eliminate weak players** — Taking out a player gives you their cards, potentially triggering a massive army surge.
- **Card escalation** — Late-game card trade-ins are worth huge armies; timing trades is critical.
- **Alliance politics** — Temporary truces help, but remember only one player wins.
